body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

um I've drawn out a little bit on the
use of payload encryption as a cyber
security defense now what I'm going with
here is explaining that when we use
encryption to encrypt firmware for iot
devices that there are some assumptions
that we're making about exactly what's
going on and um the the normal approach
that we've seen in the past of saying
that we can encrypt the firmware so that
attackers can't read it has a few little
niggles in it um and and this largely
boils down to the attack model that we
have to assume with iot devices and that
attack model typically involves an
attacker with um with sample devices
that they have physical access to most
attackers against iot devices don't just
pick a random device on the internet
they typically Target a specific kind of
device obtain copies of it uh develop an
attack for that specific device and then
start deploying it on the internet and
and so that being the case uh we need to
be aware that firmware encryption in
transit is not necessarily a defense
against an attacker so that um I've
drawn that particular niggle out in the
text now and explained that really what
we're doing with firmare encryption is
protecting IP against a casual Observer
and protecting personal information
against uh large scale collect so I I
also put in a reference to uh RFC 8990
in there in that uh respect since uh the
protection again of personal IP is
probably a top priority uh given that
the internet is for end
users um and then I've put a little bit
more expansion on the uh discussion of
defense against chosen PL text attacks
this is an interesting one in a firmware
encryption scenario because in firmware
encryption um you can assume that
there's a relatively drawn out process
between the development of a firmware
and its encryption and distribution so a
chosen plain text attack is not
impossible but would probably require a
supply chain attack for
example uh next next slide
